doc.title: Equity investments under 20% — fair value through income
doc.event: Nash Co. buys 10,000 shares of Oro Inc. (a passive stake, under 20%) at $12 per share, later receives a $0.50-per-share dividend, and holds the shares at year-end when they trade at $13. Prepare all three entries.

variants.label: Buy · collect dividends · mark to market

lines.trap: No 'AFS equity' exists anymore — since ASU 2016-01, passive equity stakes are FV-through-income, full stop. Older solution manuals disagree; the current rule doesn't.

lines.label: price paid
lines.trap: Brokerage fees expense for FVNI holdings — they don't capitalize.
Under the FAIR VALUE method dividends are INCOME. (Under the equity method — next scenario — they'd shrink the investment instead. The contrast is the exam.)

lines.label: revenue when declared/received
lines.why: A passive holder's return is dividends + price changes; both run through income.
lines.trap: Never credit the investment account here — that's equity-method thinking leaking in.

lines.why: Same target discipline: adjustment balance = FV − cost.
lines.trap: Price DOWN next year? Move the adjustment to the new target — the entry is the delta, not the cumulative.

lines.account: Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Income
lines.label: to net income
lines.why: Equity securities: always income, never OCI — there is no AFS-style parking lot for stocks.
lines.trap: '—Equity' on an equity SECURITY is the trap answer: OCI treatment belongs to AFS DEBT only.

memorize.body: Passive equity (<20%, no influence): carry at FV through NET INCOME. Dividends = Dividend Revenue. No OCI option exists for equity securities.
Ownership ladder: <20% passive → fair value (income) · 20–50% influence → equity method · >50% control → consolidate. Percent is a presumption — INFLUENCE is the real test.

questions.prompt: A 5% passive stockholder receives a 5,000 dividend. The credit is…

distractors.feedback: Crediting (reducing) the INVESTMENT is the EQUITY-method rule for 20–50% influence stakes. A passive holder records Dividend Revenue — income, not a return of the investment.

accountBank.account: Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Income
Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Equity

build.scaffold: Three beats: cost in, dividends to revenue, year-end mark to income. Both decoys are 'right account, wrong method' — the whole chapter is picking the method before picking accounts.
